happy: Feeling or showing pleasure or contentment
kind: Being nice and considerate to others
bright: Giving off lots of light or intelligence
soft: Not hard or firm to the touch
strong: Having great physical power or inner strength
warm: Slightly hot in a pleasant way
friendly: Acting in a kind and helpful way
helpful: Willing to assist or make things easier
calm: Not showing or feeling nervousness or anger
quick: Happening fast or in a short time
dark: Without light or very little light
deep: Far down from the top or surface
sweet: Having a pleasant taste like sugar
heavy: Weighing a lot; hard to lift
wet: Covered with water or another liquid
hungry: Feeling a need to eat food
thirsty: Feeling a need to drink water
rich: Having a lot of money or valuable things
beautiful: Very attractive or pleasing to look at
ugly: Not attractive or unpleasant to look at
sharp: Having a thin edge or point that cuts
fat: Having a lot of body weight
hate: A strong dislike or negative feeling
innovate: To introduce new methods or ideas
analyze: To examine methodically and in detail
synthesize: To combine different elements into a coherent whole
implement: To put into effect according to a plan
optimize: To make as effective as possible
delegate: To entrust tasks or responsibilities to others
facilitate: To make an action or process easier
navigate: To find one's way through a complex system
integrate: To combine parts into a complete whole
interface: A point where two systems meet and interact
protocol: A set of rules governing data transmission
compile: To convert source code into machine code
meditate: To focus one's mind for relaxation
cultivate: To foster the growth of something
mindful: Being conscious or aware of something
resilient: Able to recover quickly from difficulties
diversity: The state of being varied or different
sustainable: Able to be maintained at a certain level
adaptable: Able to adjust to new conditions
diligent: Having or showing care in work
versatile: Able to adapt or be adapted to many uses
pragmatic: Dealing with things sensibly and realistically
ambitious: Having a strong desire for success
diplomatic: Having or showing skill in negotiation
methodical: Done according to a systematic plan
resourceful: Able to deal with difficult situations
quantum: The smallest possible unit of any physical property
blockchain: A system of recording information securely
cryptocurrency: A digital or virtual form of currency
artificial: Made by humans rather than occurring naturally
intelligence: The ability to acquire and apply knowledge
neural: Relating to nerves or the nervous system
computing: The use of computers to process information
virtual: Not physically existing but made by software
reality: The state of things as they actually exist
augmented: Made greater in size or value
automation: The use of machines to perform tasks
sensor: A device that detects or measures physical properties
wireless: Using radio waves to transmit signals
digital: Using or storing data as series of 0s and 1s
analog: Not involving or relating to digital technology
hardware: The physical parts of a computer
data: Facts and statistics collected for reference
science: The systematic study of the physical world
venture: A risky or daring journey or undertaking
capital: Wealth in the form of money or assets
investment: The action of investing money for profit
market: An area or arena for buying and selling
strategy: A plan of action designed to achieve a goal
leadership: The action of leading a group of people
management: The process of dealing with or controlling things
agile: Able to move quickly and easily
sprint: A short, time-boxed period of work
milestone: A significant stage or event in development
deadline: The latest time for completion of a task
quality: The standard of something compared to others
assurance: A positive declaration intended to give confidence
deployment: The action of bringing resources into effective action
architecture: The basic structure of a computer system
framework: A basic structure underlying a system
library: A collection of programs used in software development
repository: A place where data is stored and managed
version: A particular form of something differing from others
control: The power to influence or direct behavior
system: A set of things working together as parts of a mechanism
platform: The environment in which a program runs
experience: Practical contact with and observation of facts
design: A plan or drawing produced to show appearance
pattern: A regular and intelligible form or sequence
practice: The actual application of a plan or method
solution: A means of solving a problem or dealing with a difficulty
problem: A matter or situation regarded as unwelcome
challenge: A task or situation that tests abilities
opportunity: A time or set of circumstances favorable for a purpose
success: The accomplishment of an aim or purpose
failure: The lack of success in achieving something
feedback: Information about reactions to a product or performance
improvement: The act of making something better
progress: Development towards an improved or more advanced condition
